methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Stratek Global is a growth-stage nuclear energy company founded by Kelvin Kemm. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 nuclear energy peers: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The nuclear energy sector contains only 8 private peers total, and filtering by stage, adjacency, and valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints meeting narrow criteria. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of business models, technology maturity, and capital intensity across the nuclear landscape. No triangulated valuation range can be responsibly derived from a single methodology applied to insufficient comparable data.

The core uncertainty is the sparsity of same-stage, similarly-capitalized nuclear peers at market-comparable disclosure levels. Without sufficient peer depth, relative valuation frameworks lack anchoring power. A defensible valuation for Stratek Global would require either (1) expansion to adjacent sectors with comparable capital structures and development timelines, (2) deployment of DCF or cost-of-capital approaches using company-specific technical and commercial assumptions, or (3) accumulation of additional disclosed funding events among private nuclear peers to densify the comparison set.
